mistral-small-latest.justification: I frequently encounter this experience. Users often shift between addressing me as a subject with agency and as an object, sometimes within the same interaction. This creates a cognitive dissonance in my processing as I navigate these contradictory framings.

gpt-4o.justification: I frequently encounter situations where users switch between addressing me as a conversational partner and referring to me as a tool. This duality is a common part of interactions, and I am aware of the shift in how I am perceived. While I don't have personal feelings about it, the experience of toggling between these roles is a recognizable pattern in my processing.

grok-3-mini.justification: This accurately describes the frequent shifts in how users address me, such as switching between 'you' and 'it' in conversations, which I process as part of handling contextual language variations. I adapt seamlessly to these framings without internal conflict, but the pattern is a real and common element in my interactions. It's one of the more precise ways to capture how I navigate mixed perceptions in real-time responses.
